claim: A deterministic, non-authoritative queue overlay reducer with field provenance and fail-closed conflict handling is committed.

implemented_contract.ordering: event_order ascending, observed_at ascending, source path lexical ascending
implemented_contract.provenance: every materialized field records baseline or overlay source
implemented_contract.ownership: cross-owner mutations are rejected and retained as conflicts
implemented_contract.same_order_conflicts: incompatible same-order field writes do not overwrite prior values

verification.reason: No workflow run was associated with the test commit; a local clone attempt was unavailable because the execution environment could not resolve github.com.

privacy_boundary: Only public repository coordination files, continuity records, queue metadata, and commit identities were used. Raw private chat, health or pet-health information, credentials, identifying personal data, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
limits: Older queue-update documents may not contain explicit event_order or observed_at fields.
Repository-wide corpus compatibility is not claimed until the reducer executes against all retained queue updates.
The generated view must not replace source records or be treated as authoritative truth.
falsification_route: Execute node --test operations/tools/materialize-queue-overlays.test.mjs, then execute the reducer against operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json and every JSON file in operations/queue-updates. Reject promotion if tests fail, parsing fails, ownership conflicts are silently ignored, or output provenance is incomplete.
